title: helianthemum vineale subsp. marifolium
intro: Helianthemum vineale subsp. marifolium, also known as rockrose, is a type of flowering plant in the Cistaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Helianthemum vineale subsp. marifolium include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, such as colds, fever, and digestive issues.
